title: Dynamic Venezuelan Corn Empanadas
description: Earthy and crisp empanadas with a vibrant corn filling, made egg‑free with self‑raising flour and a savory spice blend. They’re energizing, perfect for a quick snack.
instructions: In a mixing bowl combine self‑raising flour and 1 tsp salt.
Add warm water and 30 ml olive oil, stirring until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 2 minutes until smooth, then cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
In a saucepan, sauté diced onion in 1 tsp oil until translucent, then add minced garlic, cumin, and paprika, cooking for 1 minute.
Stir in corn kernels, chopped cilantro, 1/2 tsp salt, and pepper, cooking until corn is heated through, about 5 minutes; set aside to cool.
Divide the rested dough into 8 equal portions and roll each into a circle about 15 cm in diameter.
Place a heaping spoonful of the corn filling in the center of each dough circle, fold over to form a half‑moon, and seal the edges with a fork.
Heat a skillet to 190°C (375°F) and lightly oil it; fry the empanadas until golden brown on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
Alternatively bake at 200°C (400°F) on a parchment‑lined tray for 20 minutes, flipping halfway, until crisp.
Let the empanadas cool for a few minutes before serving warm.
